registration.verdict: VERDICT 043 — fishing economy tuning (per-species sell/reward curve + fishing progression curve)

order.source: idea-engine control/inbox.md @ 8218d6630f53633461d993d9a3caa4ad54ab251d, ORDER 006 · 2026-07-13T09:02Z, SUPERBOT-GAMES BALANCE item 5
order.verbatim: (5) FISHING-ECONOMY-TUNING — (a) pin a per-species sell/reward curve (a catch grants NO XP/currency today — games/fishing/inventory/adapter.py catch_to_grant leaves ProgressionDelta empty; key the curve on species.py size_rank/rarity_weight); (b) pin a fishing progression curve (no fishing-owned skill/level axis). Fishing is IN-REPO sim-pinned: re-run games/fishing/sim/catch_sim.py under new targets per docs/design/fishing-catch-skeleton.md §5 — never hand-edit a weight;

b0_validity_gate.rule: ALL B0 checks must pass against the byte-copied code BEFORE any tuning arm is evaluated; any B0 failure ends the verdict there (it rules on the failed premise and stops).

checks.B0.1_empty_progression: adapter.catch_to_grant(Catch(species_id, size)).progression == ProgressionDelta() for every species; ProgressionDelta() defaults are global_xp=0, game_xp=0, currency=0, capability=None; and the adapter docstring carries verbatim 'Progression is left EMPTY: fishing's resolver defines no XP/currency for a catch today' (packet quote).
checks.B0.2_species_table: species.py rows exactly: minnow size_rank=1 rarity_weight=50.0 · bass 2/30.0 · pike 3/15.0 · legend_carp 4/5.0; MAX_SIZE_RANK==4 (packet: 'key the curve on species.py size_rank/rarity_weight').
checks.B0.3_catch_shape: Catch dataclass fields exactly (species_id, size) — packet: 'The frozen Catch carries only species_id + size'.
checks.B0.4_economy_constants: catch.CAST_COST==2; energy.DIG_COST==1, energy.REGEN_SECONDS==10, energy.MAX_ENERGY==60; rewards.BASE_ROLL_MAX==2; rewards.ORE_WEIGHTS=={stone:3,bronze:2.5,iron:2,silver:1.5,gold:1,diamond:0.5}; items values stone 1/bronze 2/iron 3/silver 4/gold 6/diamond 12 (market.sell_price reads items.value for RESOURCE).

mining_faucet_grounding.cite: games/mining/core/energy.py (MAX_ENERGY=60, DIG_COST=1, REGEN_SECONDS=10 → 360 energy/hour sustained) + games/mining/core/rewards.py (BASE_ROLL_MAX=2 → base roll randint(1,2); ORE_WEIGHTS at depth 0; mine_multiplier fresh=1.0, diamond-tool ceiling ×1.5 per its own comment) + games/mining/core/items.py sell values via games/mining/core/market.py sell_price — all @ 57f69be
mining_faucet_grounding.shared_energy_pool: fishing casts debit the SAME energy engine (catch.py: 'A cast spends this through the reused games.mining.core.energy engine, so fishing shares the one energy economy'); therefore the comparable unit is COINS PER ENERGY, not coins per action.

derived_exact.mining_fresh_depth0_coins_per_energy: 32/7 ≈ 4.5714 (the parity anchor; a fresh player is depth-0-locked per ORDER 006 item 4a: max_accessible_depth==0 until a torch)
derived_exact.house_style_note: mining's own table is near-constant weight×value (3,5,6,6,6,6) — 'commonness is the inverse of worth' (rewards.py comment); the candidate fishing curve adopts exactly this inverse-rarity shape.
derived_exact.design_cue: catch.py CAST_COST comment verbatim: 'Kept modest — fishing is a slower, calmer faucet than digging' → the parity band is centered at-or-below 1.0× mining, never above.

candidate_sell_curve.family: sell(species) = round_half_even(c × 50 / rarity_weight) — pure inverse-rarity (the mining house style), keyed on the B0.2 table; monotone in size_rank because rarity_weight is inversely ordered with size_rank in the pinned table
candidate_sell_curve.calibration_rule: c is the integer in [1..20] whose fresh@dock coins/energy (measured on the main-leg re-run) is closest to the parity anchor 32/7 WITHOUT exceeding 1.05×32/7; ties break low (the calmer-faucet cue). The runner evaluates this rule mechanically; if the rule's pick differs from candidate_c the RULE wins and the discrepancy is disclosed.
candidate_sell_curve.semantics: sell values are per-fish COIN SELL PRICES for a future market/sell leg (the mining-parity shape: ore mints nothing at dig, coins enter at market.sell_price = items.value) — i.e. the future fish ItemDef rows' value field via the documented items.register_fish_species injection point; catch_to_grant currency stays 0 at catch time.

acceptance_bands.A1_parity_anchor: fresh tier @ dock (the pinned neutral identity spot) coins/energy ∈ [0.85, 1.05] × 32/7 = [3.885714..., 4.8]
acceptance_bands.A2_gear_gradient: coins/energy strictly increasing fresh < geared < master at EVERY spot (gear must pay on the coin axis)
acceptance_bands.A3_ceiling_containment: max over all 9 (spot, tier) cells of coins/energy ≤ 755/56 (fishing's best case never out-earns mining's own diamond-tool depth-4 ceiling — the shared energy pool stays allocation-stable)
acceptance_bands.A4_place_gradient: per tier, coins/energy tide_pool < dock < deep_water (the stingy/rare biome pays per energy — risk/patience is rewarded, mirroring §5b's ordered gradients)
acceptance_bands.A5_integer_sanity: all four sell values are integers ≥ 1, strictly increasing in size_rank

candidate_progression_curve.xp_per_catch: game_xp = size_rank (1/2/3/4) carried in ProgressionDelta.game_xp — the magnitude the adapter already relays as ItemMeta.value=size_rank; global_xp stays 0 (quest-owned per the exploration TIER_CAPS pattern); currency stays 0 at catch (see sell-curve semantics)
candidate_progression_curve.level_thresholds: xp_to_next(L) = 50 × L for L ≥ 1 (arithmetic ladder); cumulative XP to reach level L = 25 × L × (L−1)
candidate_progression_curve.milestones: fishing level 10 and level 25 as title-surface milestones — mirrors games/mining/core/titles.py _VETERAN_LEVEL=10 / _LEGEND_LEVEL=25 @ 57f69be
candidate_progression_curve.stat_neutrality: levels grant NO fishing_power/bite_luck — the pinned gear caps (MAX_FISHING_POWER 6 / MAX_BITE_LUCK 3) and every §5 sim bound stay untouched; progression is a recognition/milestone axis, stat growth stays gear-only (no-pay-to-win preserved, zero re-tuning of pinned weights)

acceptance_bands.P1_first_felt_level: cumulative XP of TWO full 60-energy bursts (2 × 30 casts) fresh @ dock ≥ threshold_1 = 50 (first level-up inside two sessions — the V038 F-probe early-feltness spirit)
acceptance_bands.P2_xp_gear_gradient: XP/hour strictly increasing fresh < geared < master at every spot
acceptance_bands.P3_longtail_sanity: hours to level 25 sustained master @ deep_water ∈ [24, 120] (the V038 idle-scale long tail: its measured award-20 sat at ≈39 h)

decision_rule.order: B0 first (any fail → verdict rules on the failed premise, STOP). Then: (i) sell arm APPROVES with constants iff A1–A5 all pass on the main leg AND A1–A4 reproduce on the robustness leg; (ii) progression arm APPROVES with constants iff P1–P3 all pass on the main leg AND P1–P3 reproduce on the robustness leg; (iii) any band failure → that arm goes NULL naming the failed band and the missing design input; (iv) verdict = approve-with-constants if both arms approve, conditional if exactly one, null if neither.
decision_rule.no_weight_edits: the runner drives games/fishing/sim/catch_sim.py ONLY through its public entry point run(seeds=…, spots=…, casts_per_spot=…) per skeleton §5; ZERO edits to any weight/constant in the copied modules — the curves are a fold over the sim's output distributions, exactly the 'new targets' §5 re-run the order names.

run_protocol.main_leg: catch_sim.run() at the packet's own pinned §5 protocol — seeds range(0,400), all three spots, 40 casts/spot (48,000 casts/tier). These seed values are the packet's committed protocol constants (skeleton §5: 'seeds 0–399'), re-run verbatim per the order; they are NOT fresh registry draws.
run_protocol.robustness_leg: catch_sim.run(seeds=range(20260881, 20261281)) — 400 FRESH cast-seeds drawn by this verdict, every value strictly above the fleet seed high-water 20260880 (V040); new high-water 20261280. Bands A1–A4 and P1–P3 must reproduce their main-leg pass/fail verdicts; c is NOT re-fit here.
run_protocol.byte_identity: the full runner executes twice as separate processes; stdout and results.json must be byte-identical by external diff (sha256 recorded).

gaps_disclosed.G1: no live fishing earn-rate telemetry exists anywhere (sim-lab's standing recurring-evidence gap, docs/current-state.md) — the parity frame is model-true engine arithmetic, not measured player behaviour
gaps_disclosed.G2: the sell leg does not exist in code yet (no fish ItemDef rows, no fishing sell action) — these constants are the sim-backed numbers the seam will wire VERBATIM per the packet's own ask
gaps_disclosed.G3: what a fishing LEVEL gates is a design decision deliberately NOT made here (stat-neutral proposal); if the seat later wants levels to grant stats, that change re-opens the pinned §5 bounds and needs its own sim
gaps_disclosed.G4: cooked-fish energy refill (energy.py: 'cooked fish' tops the bar) creates a potential energy-loop coupling once fish are sellable vs cookable — flagged as the named follow-up axis, out of this verdict's registered scope
